title: Untitled Slaughterhouse Thriller
logline: On the night of a lucrative Christmas Eve pig sale, a slaughterhouse crew becomes trapped inside with a vengeful employee seeking retribution after his beloved dog is accidentally killed in the production line.
antagonistOrConflict: Elio (the deaf butcher seeking revenge after his dog Guava's death) and Knox (opportunistic thief who escalates the violence)
tone: Visceral, claustrophobic, and morally ambiguous—mixing working-class realism with survival horror
themes: The violence inherent in systems of production

actBreaks.description: The crew discovers they are locked inside the slaughterhouse after the successful sale, transitioning from workplace drama to survival thriller.

validation.ruling: Scene 37 (p25, 22.9%) serves as both inciting incident and Act 1 break: Guava's slaughter is the irreversible twist that fundamentally changes the story from workplace drama to survival thriller, forcing all characters into new high-stakes conflict they cannot escape.

actBreaks.description: Knox reveals himself and his robbery motive while Elio's rampage reaches its peak, shifting focus from pure survival to a three-way conflict between Sarina, Knox, and Elio.

validation.ruling: Scene 86 (p61, 56%) functions as Act 2 break: G.W. abandons the crew after the shooting/water incident, Violetta's death is processed, and the crew commits to the dangerous plan of killing the pig for the phone—this collective decision to take lethal action marks an irreversible escalation into Act 3's final confrontations.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script operates as a pressure-cooker thriller with an unusual dual-antagonist structure that emerges organically. Act One (scenes 1-37, pp. 2-27) establishes Sarina's inherited slaughterhouse, workplace tensions, and crucially, Guava's accidental death—a 23-page slow burn that prioritizes character and moral stakes over genre mechanics. The lockdown at scene 37 initiates Act Two (scenes 38-86, pp. 28-61), which comprises the bulk of the script: a cat-and-mouse survival sequence where Elio hunts the crew in darkness. The structure becomes genuinely interesting when Knox is revealed at scene 86—the script doesn't follow a traditional midpoint revelation but instead introduces its second antagonist at the two-thirds mark, creating a chaotic three-way endgame. Act Three (scenes 87-135, pp. 67-109) is a 42-page extended climax that refuses easy resolution, segueing into an epilogue that tracks the aftermath across multiple characters. The structure feels less like a three-act screenplay and more like a novella: patient setup, extended sustained terror, messy denouement.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately uneven in ways that serve the story's thematic interests. The first 27 pages move at workplace-drama speed, with scene 5-12 (the Guava incident) given unusual narrative weight for what is technically the inciting incident. The lockdown at scene 37 doesn't accelerate the pace immediately—instead, scenes 38-52 maintain tension through character dynamics and failed escape attempts. True momentum kicks in at scene 56 (p. 41) when the crew splits up, and the script sustains breakneck intensity from scenes 67-86 (pp. 67-61), a stretch dominated by cross-cutting and SAME TIME notation. The Knox reveal at scene 86 creates a brief lull, then the final 20 pages (scenes 114-135) oscillate between confrontation, resolution, and an extended six-scene epilogue (scenes 131-135) that refuses catharsis. The 135-scene count with 1.2-page average suggests heavy fragmentation during the siege sequences—a structural choice that generates disorientation and panic.

incitingIncident.description: Carlito slits Guava's throat and belly as Elio enters and witnesses it, letting out a booming scream before cradling her dead body while an unnatural tension builds in him and Carlito realizes with horror what he's done.

validation.ruling: Scene 14 has no content provided for evaluation. Scene 37 (p25, 22.9%) is the clear inciting incident: Carlito accidentally slaughters Elio's beloved pig Guava, shattering the status quo and triggering Elio's violent transformation that drives the entire thriller premise forward—this irreversible act forces all characters into survival mode.

midpoint.description: The lights go out, causing chaos as Pepe falls and injures his leg, and when Zane and Carlito rescue him from the sty, they discover Lazaro's severed leg being devoured by the pigs.

validation.ruling: Scene 67 (p45, 41.3%) functions as a strong midpoint with multiple markers: the discovery of Lazaro's severed leg raises stakes dramatically, the phone in the pig becomes a crucial new objective (shifting crew from purely reactive to proactive problem-solving), and Violetta's death mentioned creates a point of no return for the trapped characters.

climax.description: Elio enters the office and throws Knox across the room, begins to free Sarina who urges him to leave before police arrive, but Knox stabs Elio in the hand and Elio lifts Knox by the throat as police arrive and order him to freeze.

validation.ruling: Scene 128 (p99, 90.8%) is the definitive climax: police arrival creates maximum tension, Elio's final confrontation with Knox over Sarina reaches peak intensity, the knife fight and destruction of Elio's hearing aid represent the ultimate obstacle, and Elio's choice to stay despite consequences demonstrates complete character arc resolution.

scenes.heading: INT. CABALLERO FUNERAL HOME - SALON B - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sarina meets with a shady lawyer at her father's funeral, learning that her father doesn't want to sell the slaughterhouse despite the financial burden, which causes her to well up with emotion.

scenes.heading: INT. CABALLERO FUNERAL HOME - HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sarina walks through the funeral home hallway.

scenes.heading: INT. CABALLERO FUNERAL HOME - RESTROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sarina freshens her makeup in the restroom, willing her tough exterior to emerge.

scenes.heading: INT. CABALLERO FUNERAL HOME - SALON A - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Isabel expresses sympathy to Sarina at the funeral while Sarina deflects and quickly sends her to see the lawyer, maintaining emotional distance.

scenes.heading: INT. PIG SLAUGHTERHOUSE - HOLDING AREA - DAY
scenes.summary: Elio searches frantically for Guava in the slaughterhouse holding area while mourners pay respects at the funeral.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - MAIN CORRIDOR - DAY
scenes.summary: Elio reads lips as someone whispers insults about him, jolts back to his search, then brings Guava to Raul's casket where she lets out a mournful shriek that startles the funeral attendees.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - BUTCHERING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Elio butchers a pig carcass with precision while Guava watches and plays with her red rubber ball, pausing to play fetch with her.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Security monitors show Elio sharpening a knife on a pedal-powered stone wheel as sparks fly.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - MAIN CORRIDOR - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Pepe makes a crude comment about someone being on their phone all the time.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - MEZZANINE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Sarina looks down from the mezzanine as Ivanka warns her about the blood, and Sarina glares at her father's portrait in the office, sarcastically thanking him.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - LOUNGE - DAY
scenes.summary: Isabel hangs a Christmas ornament with her and Raul's photo while Lazaro and Violetta flirt in the lounge.

scenes.heading: INT. MEZZANINE - DAY
scenes.summary: Sarina announces over the PA system that phones will be confiscated daily after discovering leaked undercover footage, angering the crew who see it as communist control despite Isabel trying to calm them.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sarina establishes authoritarian control, creating resentment among workers and setting up conflict

scenes.heading: EXT. RURAL ROAD - DAY
scenes.summary: Sarina hammers a 'Noche Buena Pig, Cash Only' sign at the slaughterhouse entrance while two unseen men in a pickup truck with a Confederate flag bumper sticker watch her from across the street.

scenes.heading: INT. BUTCHERING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Guava's red ball rolls out of the butchering room into the corridor and she follows it.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Guava trails her red ball through the corridor.

scenes.heading: INT. BUTCHERING ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Zane aggressively throws Guava's ball at her to scare her away, then mocks Elio by telling him to keep his 'bitch on a leash' when Elio retrieves Guava.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN ENTRANCE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elio signs to Guava that outside is dangerous and she must always stay with him, then Sarina hands back employees' phones as they leave with Elio last in line.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUS STOP - DAY
scenes.summary: Young Sarina asks Elio what he sees and he responds in Spanish that he sees two beautiful honey-colored things, referring to her eyes.

scenes.heading: INT. BUS - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Elio gives Sarina an unexpected childlike hug that takes her aback, then says goodnight before leaving with Guava.

scenes.heading: EXT. SARINA'S FATHER'S HOUSE - DRIVEWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elio and Guava board a bus while Sarina arrives at her father's house with a 'For Sale' sign and notices a weathered tire swing.

scenes.heading: INT. SARINA'S FATHER'S HOUSE - BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sarina tosses her father's clothes on the bed while on speaker phone trying to sell the slaughterhouse, finding a squashed cigarette pack in his suit pocket and throwing it away.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sarina watches old Hi-8 tapes of her mother singing at her birthday party and later in a hospital bed, but the footage is interrupted by warbled static revealing Isabel and Raul kissing on New Year's, recorded over her mother's moments.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sarina discovers Isabel and Raul's relationship was documented over precious memories of her dying mother, deepening her resentment

scenes.heading: INT. ELIO'S TRAILER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elio shares food with Guava in his trailer adorned with children's crayon drawings while ignoring Knox's condescending comments about Cubans and pigs, until Knox gives him a paternal touch with his rattlesnake-tattooed hand.

scenes.heading: EXT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - MAIN ENTRANCE - MORNING
scenes.summary: Knox and G.W. leave Elio's trailer as Elio pulls Guava close.

scenes.heading: INT. MEZZANINE STAIRS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lazaro throws his phone into the bag that Sarina collects and ascends the mezzanine stairs.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Sarina locks the bag of phones in a cabinet in the office.

scenes.heading: EXT. REAR ACCESS DOORWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Pepe in a Santa hat takes orders from a long line of customers at the rear access doorway, including a male customer and his young boy ordering seventy pounds of pig again.

scenes.heading: INT. PIG STY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lazaro wrangles a 90-pound pig into the weigh station where it gets tagged and sent for processing.

scenes.heading: EXT. CASHIER BOOTH - DAY
scenes.summary: Violetta flirts with the young boy at the cashier booth, collecting two hundred dollars and stamping the order slip as paid.

scenes.heading: EXT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The male customer and young boy walk away from the cashier booth with their paid order slip.

scenes.heading: INT. BUTCHERING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Lazaro requests a pig leg and four feet from Elio, who gives a thumbs up and begins hacking off a leg from the carcass.

scenes.heading: INT. BUTCHERING ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Elio hacks off an entire pig leg as Lazaro instructed.

scenes.heading: INT. PIG STY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Guava's red ball bounces out of the butchering room and she follows it down the steps into the receiving bay.

scenes.heading: INT. RECEIVING BAY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Guava squeezes into the pig sty to retrieve her red ball but her collar gets caught on a screw, and when she yanks it hard the collar snaps off.

scenes.heading: INT. PROCESSING AREA - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Elio asks Lazaro if he's seen Guava, but Lazaro says he hasn't and is too busy to help.

scenes.heading: INT. PIG STY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Zane hoses Guava down and attaches electrode pincers to her head, then struggles to turn on the malfunctioning Pig Stunning Machine before plugging in the loose, taped power cord that shoots sparks.

scenes.heading: INT. PROCESSING ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Carlito slits Guava's throat and belly as Elio enters and witnesses it, letting out a booming scream before cradling her dead body while an unnatural tension builds in him and Carlito realizes with horror what he's done.
scenes.turningPointNote: Guava's accidental slaughter triggers Elio's violent breakdown, the inciting incident that sets the revenge plot in motion

scenes.heading: EXT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Sarina confronts Elio at dusk, shoving a bloody bag with Guava's body into his chest and angrily signing that he has no self-control, while Elio insists they killed his friend and Sarina fires him.

scenes.heading: INT. BUS - MOVING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elio sits despondent on the bus holding the bag with Guava's dead body while an elderly woman stares at him.

scenes.heading: EXT. ELIO'S TRAILER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elio digs a hole in the rain, gently places Guava's body inside, fills the grave, and creates a small makeshift memorial with her red ball.

scenes.heading: INT. DUECE'S - DIVE BAR - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Knox pitches G.W. on robbing the slaughterhouse during the Christmas party, claiming they have over a hundred thousand dollars and framing it as taking back what's rightfully theirs from the immigrants who pushed them out.
scenes.turningPointNote: Knox and G.W. plan the robbery that will trigger the night's violent events

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sarina tells Isabel she'll drop the cash at the bank and cut out early, but after Isabel leaves, Sarina secretly retrieves the bag of confiscated phones from the locked cabinet.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - FREEZER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sarina talks on the phone in the freezer about obtaining money while the employees' phones slowly sink into a vat of pig fat, then accidentally elbows her own phone into the vat when startled by Isabel entering.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Isabel sneaks out of the lounge as the tired crew drinks and parties, with Carlito bloodied and bruised from Elio's attack.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE - LATER
scenes.summary: Isabel brings Sarina coquito and reminisces about finding Sarina's childhood tutu, trying to connect over good memories, but Sarina abruptly ends the conversation and storms off to return the phones.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCKER ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Ivanka exits a bathroom stall and heads back to the lounge.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Lazaro and Violetta have sex in the dark hallway when a cleaver suddenly digs deep into Lazaro's exposed back, causing him to bulge his eyes in shock while Violetta remains oblivious, moaning with her eyes closed.
scenes.turningPointNote: The violent attack begins as someone starts systematically killing the slaughterhouse workers

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A large male figure presses his knee on Lazaro's throat and hacks off his right leg with a cleaver while Lazaro screams in agony and begs him to stop.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sarina attempts to unlock the office safe when panicked screams from the lounge make her stop, take a breath, and relock it before rushing to investigate the bloody, frenzied Violetta being comforted by the crew.

scenes.heading: EXT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE MAIN DOOR - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The crew reacts to the attack at the main door of the slaughterhouse.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - LOUNGE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The crew discovers they're locked in the lounge when a metal fragment blocks the door, and a severely wounded Lazaro drags himself inside missing his right leg while Violetta cowers in shame.
scenes.turningPointNote: Lazaro's mutilated return reveals the severity of the threat and traps the crew in the lounge.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tensions flare as Isabel and Sarina argue while the crew debates who attacked Lazaro, and Zane rallies everyone to try ramming the door with a table.

scenes.heading: INT. HONDA CIVIC - MOVING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brief transition in moving Honda Civic.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROAD
scenes.summary: Brief exterior road transition.

scenes.heading: INT. HONDA CIVIC - MOVING
scenes.summary: Isabel initially wants to wait for rescue, but Sarina insists on retrieving the phones and Isabel agrees she can't go alone.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jean leaves a voicemail for Violetta while retrieving a mechanical toy pig from his car as Carlito, Zane, and Pepe cautiously enter the slaughterhouse.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE
scenes.summary: In the office, Pepe hears a faint ringtone from the pigsty while Carlito monitors security cameras and Sarina feigns surprise that the phones are missing from the cabinet.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sarina's deception about the phones reveals her hidden agenda while the ringtone leads toward discovering the truth.

scenes.heading: INT. STY
scenes.summary: Brief transition to sty.

scenes.heading: INT. STY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Brief sty moment.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Pepe investigates the ringing phone among the pigs while Sarina begins working on the safe and Carlito reluctantly leaves to tell the others the phones are gone.

scenes.heading: INT. STY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Brief sty continuation.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Brief office continuation.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE STY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Brief sty continuation.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Pepe searches through the pigs for the ringing phone while Sarina spots a dark figure darting past on the security monitor and grabs a letter opener from the desk.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Brief office moment.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE STY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Brief sty moment.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The lights go out, causing chaos as Pepe falls and injures his leg, and when Zane and Carlito rescue him from the sty, they discover Lazaro's severed leg being devoured by the pigs.
scenes.turningPointNote: The blackout escalates the threat level and the discovery of Lazaro's leg confirms the pigs' gruesome role.

scenes.heading: INT. MEZZANINE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Isabel takes attendance and realizes Sarina is missing as Pepe reveals he found Lazaro's leg being eaten by the pigs, while Sarina clutches her letter opener and creeps out of the office into the darkened slaughterhouse.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE
scenes.summary: Brief slaughterhouse moment.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE
scenes.summary: Brief lounge moment.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sarina spots a male figure with a metallic object, panics and races through the slaughterhouse slipping on Lazaro's blood before pounding desperately on the lounge door to be let in.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: The crew debates whether Elio is behind the attacks, with Violetta dismissing the idea while Sarina volunteers to restore power and Zane asserts his authority by taking charge of the mission with Carlito as backup.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Carlito questions whether Elio would cut off his own leg for money while Zane reveals his skepticism, and Isabel prepares candles as Sarina searches cabinets in the lounge.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE
scenes.summary: Isabel discovers Sarina has mixed bleach and glass cleaner creating toxic gas, dumps the mixture and confronts Sarina about acting alone while the crew coughs from the fumes.

scenes.heading: EXT. MAIN GATE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: As Zane flips the electrical switch, Elio appears from the darkness and cleaves Zane's hand clean off with a massive cleaver, while Carlito witnesses in horror.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elio's violent attack confirms him as the threat and severely injures Zane, marking the first direct confrontation.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Knox and G.W. prepare to break into the slaughterhouse with masks and a shotgun as Zane and Carlito burst into the lounge with Zane's severed hand, and the crew learns Elio is responsible while Sarina reveals his history of violent rage triggered by trauma.
scenes.turningPointNote: Knox and G.W.'s arrival introduces a second external threat while Sarina's backstory explains Elio's dangerous psychology.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE ROOFTOP ACCESS LADDER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sarina recounts how Elio brutally beat neighborhood kids who destroyed his model plane and how his sweet demeanor returned only after she helped him repair it, revealing his violent dissociative episodes.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brief office transition.

scenes.heading: INT. MEZZANINE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brief mezzanine transition.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Knox realizes he forgot the drill and sends G.W. back to the truck, while Sarina convinces Isabel they must retrieve Lazaro's burner phone from the pigsty despite the danger.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Brief slaughterhouse transition.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Lazaro confesses to Violetta that he was paid off and wanted to use the money to run away with her, and she gives him a conflicted kiss before leaving, while G.W. trips on the pig shocking device cord causing exposed wires to fall on the floor.

scenes.heading: INT. PICK-UP TRUCK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brief pickup truck transition.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE STY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: G.W. removes his mask to scratch his face and pockets the truck keys while Sarina, Ivanka, and Violetta search the pigsty finding Lazaro's mauled leg but not the phone, until they hear the muffled ringtone and continue searching.

scenes.heading: EXT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Brief exterior transition.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: G.W. fires his gun into the darkness and falls through the rear access door as it slams shut, his stray bullet hits a water canister that flows toward exposed wires, Violetta is electrocuted trying to catch the Phone Pig, and the crew mourns as Sarina rallies them to retrieve the phone from the pig despite Knox's presence.
scenes.turningPointNote: Violetta's electrocution death raises the stakes catastrophically and the Phone Pig becomes a crucial MacGuffin.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE
scenes.summary: Elio brutally attacks the crew with meat hooks, slicing Isabel's back as Lazaro bites Elio's leg before Elio kicks Lazaro's jaw apart, and Elio destroys the stereo playing familiar Christmas music in a tearful rage while Jean discovers Violetta's electrocuted body.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Christmas music triggers Elio's emotional breakdown and Lazaro is horrifically mutilated in the escalating violence.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE
scenes.summary: Brief office transition.

scenes.heading: INT. FREEZER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Carlito and Jean carry the injured Pepe away from Violetta's body while Zane's skin blisters from burns, and Knox locks himself in the office to bandage his hook wound while monitoring the security cameras.

scenes.heading: INT. LOCKER ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sarina desperately tries multiple phones from the freezer but they're all dead or broken, and Isabel realizes Sarina's deception as Ivanka accidentally shatters her perfume bottle while grabbing rollerblades, then hides in her locker as Elio's shadow appears.
scenes.turningPointNote: All phones being broken eliminates the crew's hope for rescue and Isabel discovers Sarina's betrayal.

scenes.heading: INT. STY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Brief sty transition.

scenes.heading: INT. CLOSET - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Brief closet transition.

scenes.heading: INT. FREEZER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Ivanka limps away with her rollerblades as Carlito, Pepe, and Jean hide among the pigs while Jean has a crying breakdown about Violetta and Elio tracks the commotion in the sty.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE
scenes.summary: Brief slaughterhouse transition.

scenes.heading: INT. MEZZANINE
scenes.summary: Isabel tells Sarina a pig joke to comfort her, and Sarina admits her father was right not to trust her while Isabel insists he never stopped loving her despite their complicated relationship.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE
scenes.summary: Brief slaughterhouse transition.

scenes.heading: INT. FREEZER
scenes.summary: Brief freezer transition.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE
scenes.summary: Brief office transition.

scenes.heading: INT. FREEZER
scenes.summary: Elio pulls the freezer door open against the ratchet strap as Sarina and Isabel desperately hold it shut, with Sarina confessing she hated Isabel for taking her father away while Knox spills ammunition trying to reload with his injured hand.

scenes.heading: INT. CLOSET
scenes.summary: Brief closet transition.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE
scenes.summary: The slaughterhouse door begins to open as light breaks into the freezer.

scenes.heading: INT. FREEZER
scenes.summary: Isabel collapses and releases her grip on the strap while Sarina screams she can't hold the door alone, and Ivanka extracts a bloody glass shard from her foot.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE
scenes.summary: Action continues outside the freezer in the slaughterhouse.

scenes.heading: INT. FREEZER
scenes.summary: Isabel orders Sarina into the vat while pulling the strap herself, and Sarina submerges into the pig fat as Elio's fingers creep through the freezer door.

scenes.heading: INT. MEZZANINE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Knox loses sight of Elio from the mezzanine as Elio enters the freezer.

scenes.heading: INT. FREEZER
scenes.summary: Isabel lures Elio into the freezer before he notices activity in the slaughterhouse, and tiny air bubbles rise from the vat where Sarina hides.

scenes.heading: INT. STY
scenes.summary: Knox fires his gun at the sty, causing the pigs to panic and disperse.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE
scenes.summary: Sarina emerges from the vat to find Isabel dead, strips off her fat-soaked clothes, while Elio grabs the flamethrower and sets Carlito's legs on fire before targeting Pepe who kisses his gold cross in realization of his imminent death.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE
scenes.summary: Action continues in the lounge area.

scenes.heading: INT. BUTCHERING ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Ivanka rollerblades through electrified water and escapes while Carlito tackles Elio, Jean pushes Elio into the butchering room, and Knox grabs Sarina who kicks him in the groin and claws at his face.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elio brutally beats Carlito's face like tenderizing meat, drives a cleaver through Carlito's forehead just as Jean hits Elio with a hammer, and Sarina emerges from the lounge with a shotgun.
scenes.turningPointNote: Carlito is killed, reducing the survivors and escalating the violence to a new level of brutality.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE
scenes.summary: Knox's voice echoes over the loudspeaker as Sarina and Jean enter the lounge to search for supplies.

scenes.heading: EXT. GAS STATION - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Sarina pulls chemical bottles and plucks a glass ornament from the Christmas tree while Knox taunts her over the loudspeaker, and Jean reveals Knox likely entered through the ceiling like he did.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Scene transitions to the slaughterhouse floor.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE
scenes.summary: Outside, police officers accidentally shoot Ivanka when a car backfires and they mistake it for gunfire, while inside Sarina enters the office with hands raised and finds Knox by the safe with a gun.
scenes.turningPointNote: Ivanka is shot by police, removing another survivor, and Knox corners Sarina in the office near the safe.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOFTOP ACCESS LADDER
scenes.summary: Knox fires at Sarina as she climbs the rooftop access ladder to escape the gas, but the ladder's bolts wrench free and she plummets, banging her head.

scenes.heading: EXT. RURAL ROAD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Scene transitions to a rural road at night.

scenes.heading: INT. AMBULANCE - MOVING - NIGHT
scenes.summary: In an ambulance, Ivanka wakes disoriented and tries to tell the paramedic to send police to the slaughterhouse.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOUNGE ENTRANCE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Knox hears Jean's footsteps on the roof while Sarina's vision blurs and she loses consciousness.

scenes.heading: EXT. CARLITO'S VAN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jean spots Carlito's van outside.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Scene returns to the office.

scenes.heading: INT. LOUNGE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Scene shifts to the lounge.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Jean breaks into Carlito's van to get a sledgehammer, while Knox interrogates bound Sarina for the safe combination and smacks her face, and Jean grabs a toy pig from the Christmas pile and wedges the lounge door shut.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE
scenes.summary: Knox slams the office door against Jean who's hiding behind it, knocking him down the stairs, then rushes into the office and cuts Sarina's bindings before Knox climbs back up.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Action continues in the slaughterhouse below.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Knox jams his finger into Sarina's bullet wound to wake her, Elio awakens downstairs and finds the toy pig which he mistakes for his beloved Guava, and Knox begins opening the safe as Jean lies unconscious.

scenes.heading: EXT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Police cars arrive at the slaughterhouse with sirens wailing.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Elio enters the office and throws Knox across the room, begins to free Sarina who urges him to leave before police arrive, but Knox stabs Elio in the hand and Elio lifts Knox by the throat as police arrive and order him to freeze.
scenes.turningPointNote: Elio confronts Knox and the police arrive, marking the climactic convergence of all story threads.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Elio twists and snaps Knox's neck despite Sarina's pleas, drops his body, and tearfully raises his hands and kneels as Sarina begs police not to shoot.
scenes.turningPointNote: Knox dies and Elio surrenders to police, resolving the primary conflict but sealing Elio's tragic fate.

scenes.heading: EXT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Outside, Jean tells a reporter that Elio killed Violetta while Sarina watches from an ambulance as Elio is placed in a police car, their eyes meeting.

scenes.heading: INT. SARINA'S CAR - MOVING - DAY
scenes.summary: Police lower their weapons as freed pigs stampede out of the slaughterhouse in a majestic moment, and Sarina locks eyes with Elio one final time from the ambulance.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Scene transitions to the slaughterhouse during daytime.

scenes.heading: INT. SLAUGHTERHOUSE OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Ivanka and Sarina drive past a busy restaurant while discussing the aftermath, Sarina opens the safe to find all the money intact and breaks down crying at her desk surrounded by photos of her deceased crew.
scenes.turningPointNote: Sarina discovers the money is safe, providing closure to one storyline and emotional resolution to the immediate aftermath.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CUBAN VEGAN RESTAURANT - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Five years later, Jean bartends at Guava's Restaurant, a vegan Cuban establishment, where Sarina's daughter draws a pig while Sarina cooks in the kitchen, having transformed the slaughterhouse money into a new life.
scenes.turningPointNote: The epilogue reveals Sarina's complete transformation from slaughterhouse manager to vegan restaurant owner, providing thematic resolution.

scenes.heading: INT. JAIL CELL - DAY
scenes.summary: In prison, Elio receives mail containing his daughter's drawing of a pig farm and pins it on his wall alongside a photo of Sarina's family at Guava's Restaurant, smiling as he admires his collection.
